description: A Roman pasta dish made by coating hot spaghetti with a sauce formed from egg, hard cheese, black pepper, and cured pork. The creamy texture comes from the emulsion rather than cream in the classic version.
optional_ingredients: Bucatini or rigatoni instead of spaghetti
preparation_steps: Cook the pasta and reserve some cooking water.
Cook the cured pork until lightly crisp and the fat renders.
Beat eggs with grated cheese and black pepper.
Toss the hot pasta with the pork, then off the heat mix in the egg-cheese mixture, loosening with pasta water if needed.
Serve immediately before the sauce over-thickens.

health_advice.risks: Often high in sodium, saturated fat, and calories; raw or lightly cooked egg requires safe handling.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use a moderate portion and balance the meal with vegetables or salad.

dietary_notes.note: Classic carbonara contains pork, eggs, and cheese. Vegetarian versions exist, but vegan versions are not traditional. Gluten-free is possible with suitable pasta.

common_variations: Versions using pancetta instead of guanciale
